text.content:
Every mission in this campaign features the following block of information. This info is intended to be shared with your players directly (just screenshot it) after they've been briefed on the job by their point of contact.

Gig Title

Friend Opportunity

Making a new friend unlocks a bonus associated with that friend (listed on the mook sheet for the Afterlife NPCs) and unlocks the ability to call on that friend with PBGPs.

Enemy Opportunity

Enemies from player backgrounds can be substituted in for enemies that are included in the modules; if a player defeats their enemy in a suitably satisfying way, they earn 1 personal rep that expires in an in-game month.

Personal Rep Opportunity

Sometimes these are high-DV checks; other times, they're just specific actions that have specific consequences - the first person to do the opportunity earns 1 personal rep.

Gig Payout

Usually a combination of Eagles, euros, and gear. IP should be awarded at GM discretion - I recommend consulting the tables in RED CORE p. 410.

In this beat, the players are introduced to their employer while being allowed into the Afterlife for the first time.

This mission makes use of the @UUID[Scene.s4clZm7hY4mfQllb]{Afterlife Lobby} and @UUID[Scene.XcdL7EiMCNgtQHHt]{6th Street Shootout} scenes, along with a few environments. To begin:

  • Place a token for @UUID[Actor.17CTAE5lKmZFHY9i]{Vido Dubois} in the Afterlife Lobby scene, sitting at some of the furniture.

  • Place the player tokens outside the front doors in the Afterlife Lobby scene

  • Change the scene to @UUID[Scene.VCbthP8Ez4uhtdkr]{Afterlife Exterior Environment}.

    Then read the following:

Exterior, 66 Lincoln Blvd, Upper Marina

66 Lincoln Blvd. isn’t much to look at from the outside. The Upper Marina was the first district in Night City to be rebuilt after the bombing of Arasaka Tower, and you’re surrounded on all sides by the glitz and glitter of Downtown Night City. The homeless still lurk in alleyways, and the local gang presence is still watching the streets, but there’s no gunfire and NCPD armored patrol cars roll by regularly, protecting the new corporate interests, there.

The front of the building you've come to see is understated and nondescript. On its left side is the entrance to an underground parking garage with its shutter closed. Beyond that is a cybernetics sales association, its front windows filled with reruns of bloody, cyber-augmented UFC matches from the last year. On the right is a high-end counseling center, its storefront and sidewalk lined with holographic emitters of beautiful, well-dressed counselors promising comfort and happiness in sincere, angelic voices. 

By comparison, your own address is so quiet that a passerby wouldn’t even notice it. A stylized, neon-red A glares with light above a pair of double doors. The building is 9 stories tall, abutting the sidewalk, and adorned by red LED strips to cast a bloody light over the street. It’s quiet, and the entrance is deserted. The windows, both on the ground level and upper floors, are all tinted, and look like they’ve been recently cleaned.

In place of door handles on the double doors, there are a pair of coin slots and a complex looking locking apparatus next to them. A door-cam strip is roughly at eye level above each mechanism. No one, and nothing else, is present save the party - the street deserted except for the occasional car or pedestrian, hurrying about in the miserable, persistent drizzle.

A DV17 Conceal/Reveal object check reveals that there is a powerful autoturret hidden in the top of the alcove above the door; once it's been located, a DV13 Elecctronics/Security Tech check reveals that it is not currently running.

The door does not open when interacted with, is locked from the inside, and will not respond to anything except the slotting of the gold coin in one of the associated slots. A DV11 Deduction check is sufficient to piece together that the coin you were given is supposed to be used for that purpose.

When you slot the coin, it vanishes into the mechanism with a satisfying mechanical ‘clunk!’ A moment later, a digital overlay appears above the lock it was slotted into, showing a face: Caucasian, late 30s at your guess, with a smoking cigarette between his lips and some military-grade cyberware installed on his cheek. His eyes glow with the telltale light of chyron implants, and you can see an army jacket and tags at the bottom of the frame. He's lounging about on a sheet-covered piece of furniture, and the glow in his eyes fades as he looks at you.

(Now would be a good time to share Vido's picture with the party - right click the mook, select "view character artwork, and then select "share with players" in the upper right corner of the resulting window.)

"Bonjour, mes amis!" he exclaims.

(if a character speaks french, it means "hello, my friends!").

"I have been waiting for you. Come in, come in, and be sure to wipe your feet, we just had the carpet installed!"

The doors slide open automatically with a hiss, revealing the building's interior. As they do, a cool, female voice says "Welcome to the Afterlife" over a hidden speaker.

(Swap the scene to Afterlife Lobby.)

Your players will likely want to make a good impression on Vido; doing so will affect their job payout as follows:

  • As players enter the hotel, have each of them make a DV15 personal grooming check which their agent bonus may apply to; if they have a base roll of 10 or more in Wardrobe & Style, this check is reduced to a DV13. Tally the group results for passes/fails.

    • If less than a third of the party failed, Vido's pay offer increases by 50eb/player.

    • If more players passed than failed, but a third or more of the party failed, nothing happens.

    • If more players failed than passed, Vido's offer is reduced by the same amount.

    • In both cases, Vido reacts amusedly and with slight disdain to those who don't look the part.

  • If any players have 0 points in personal grooming (excluding bonuses from chemskin and techhair), they dirty the carpet and furniture as they enter. Vido will become annoyed at their lack of care and reduce that particular player's EB payout by half, to pay for the damages.

Interior, Afterlife Lobby

Inside the double doors, a three-story-high hotel lobby greets you, lined with spacious seating arrangements of furniture on either side of the main walkway, all of which is covered by plastic sheets as though not yet ready for use. These side areas are partitioned by panels of reinforced, bulletproof wood and frosted bulletproof glass. At the front of the room, about 30 feet ahead, there are exits to other parts of the building on the left and right sides and the pathway inwards stops in front of an empty concierge's desk. Behind it are a pair of elevators, with a stair access between them.

A simulated dusk is the room’s primary source of light, shining a golden glow from above the elevators. Holographic projectors are managing an expanse of fluffy clouds beneath blue skies. Unless you frequent the Deep Badlands, this is likely the first time you’ve seen a sky this blue in 20 years - the coastal skies are either some light tone of gray or red, most of the time.

"Over here!" calls the voice from the door; looking to one of the room's furniture sets, the man from the doorway is lounging upon one of the tarp-covered seats and waves brightly at you as you enter. "Come, sit, welcome to what will soon be the greatest location in Night City!"

Talking As Vido

To save both of us reading time and to promote active play, Vido conveys the details of the job, in character, to your players as follows. Give the information to them through your conversation, after making pleasantries - Vido will ask after appearances, past work, and areas of expertise. If a character has a personal rep of 2 or more, or is a rockerboy or media, Vido has heard of them and their work. He will also talk a little about himself (see his mook sheet for more info) if asked.

Job details:

  • The building the players are in is soon to become the Afterlife, a highly reputable hotspot for edgerunners to meet and connect, reopening after the 2020s.

    • Unless your party's characters are particularly old, or have a fascination with edgerunning culture and its mainstays, they likely will not have heard of the Afterlife before now.

  • The security system for the building is elaborate, and will turn the hotel into a fortress once activated - but the software to run the systems has to be sourced from 6th Street, and it's "highly in demand."

    • Vido is concerned that 6th Street will double-cross the org, or that something will go wrong with the transaction - there were several other interested parties (Maelstrom, scavs, even the illusive and unknown Iron Sights) which were outbid for it.

  • The hotel's concierge and manager-in-chief has not reported back from scouting the deal.

    • The concierge's handle is "Sprinter," and he describes her as "big-mouthed, British, and unbearably perky."

    • Sprinter was meant to meet the party along with Vido, and accompany them on the job.

    • Vido shares her contact details, though she does not pick up her agent, if called.

  • He also gives the players the location of the exchange, and notes that the gear has already been paid for, and 6th Street knows they're coming.

    • "Remmy wants a roast beef sandwich" is the codephrase which will let 6th Street know the party is affiliated with Vido/the job.

    • The location is a nondescript empty lot in South Night City, well within 6th Street territory.

  • Vido's payment can be negotiated for using the fixer's haggle skill or via a trading check against a DV15 (or DV21 if using persuasion), increasing payout by 10%/40eb per edgerunner on a success. On failure, nothing happens.

Once this conversation is concluded, share the mission summary table on page 1 with your players if you haven't already, then proceed to beat 2. Be sure to note any differences in payout!

Total required setup time: 30-40 minutes.

The party receives an odd message from a stranger, with a mysterious coin in their pocket. Today, the mystery ends and they meet their first contact at the Afterlife, the hotel/bar/Night City playpen sure to be the thrumming heart of Night City's edgerunning community in just a few short weeks. Can you say "ground floor opportunity"?

Guarding The Pearly Gates

Friend Opportunity: Sprinter

Sprinter is proficient in Akido, and offers to train her friends. She takes well to "sporty" or athletic characters & interests, as well as MMA and other organized fighting, and will ask after them - she also enjoys hiking in restricted territories on her off time. She strongly dislikes brutes, flirts, and confidence (wo)men.

Enemy Opportunity: Scav Lead

The leader of the scav gang the players will be put against is an unhinged, violent psychopath - if any players have enemies which are part of a boostergang, you can replace the existing bad guy with one of them.

Rep Opportunity: Fast Hands

The first time an enemy would set foot in the Afterlife, one player with a held action may opt to make an aimed shot. If the aimed shot hits, the enemy is killed immediately instead of setting foot inside, regardless of their current health, and the player responsible gains 1 rep.

Gig Payout

400eb + 2 Eagles per player - also unlocks the Afterlife's security apparatus, making it a safehouse. A bonus of 100eb+1 Eagle/edgerunner will be provided if the Afterlife lobby's carpets stay as pristine as they were when the players first see them.

Beat Chart

Beat 1: Meeting Vido

Players meet their fixer contact, enter the Afterlife for the first time, are briefed on the job, and have the chance to ask questions.

Beat 2: Finding Sprinter

Players are sent out to meet up with Sprinter, the Afterlife's new concierge, who's gone missing on the day's assignment. They locate her and plan their next move.

Beat 3: Defeating the Scavs

The 6th Streeters Sprinter was going to meet were massacred by a scav gang that was after the job's bounty - military grade IFF and targeting soft for an array of traps and defenses. They'll need taken down.

Beat 4: Evading 6th Street

6th Street arrives on scene as the last of the scavs are dealt with, and will need to be managed.

Beat 5: In The Nick Of Time!

The players are summoned back to the Afterlife with enemy forces in hot pursuit, and must hold the lobby while the building defenses are brought online.

In this beat, the players meet Sprinter, the Afterlife's concierge, and have the opportunity to assess a moderate-sized force of scavs before engaging them.

This beat uses the @UUID[Scene.XcdL7EiMCNgtQHHt]{6th Street Shootout} scene, and an environment. To set up this scene, as well as the next beat:

  • Place a token for @UUID[Actor.C1opBA4gYUZcTiGp]{Sprinter} in the enclosed building on the east side of the 6th Street Shootout scene.

    • Sprinter has taken 5 damage, and her armor has been ablated twice.

  • Place tokens for each player character across the street near Sprinter's building, out of view of the scavs in the schoolyard.

  • Place a @UUID[Compendium.night-city-gang-and-corp-mook-pack.NCGangsnCorpsMooks.Actor.SNKnBqS4lCZPd5yU]{Scav Savage} in the same room as Sprinter, on the chair. The savage is at 5 health, and has no weapons or armor.

  • At the same building's southern entrance, in the alcove formed by the fence, place a token for 1 dead @UUID[Compendium.night-city-gang-and-corp-mook-pack.NCGangsnCorpsMooks.Actor.zeG9k6liLkdHNrf4]{Booster Gangster} and 1 dead @UUID[Compendium.night-city-gang-and-corp-mook-pack.NCGangsnCorpsMooks.Actor.SNKnBqS4lCZPd5yU]{Scav Savage}.

    • If players investigate the bodies, they find that one was throat-punched, and the other's neck was snapped.

  • In the map's main area, place the following:

    • 1 + (1*1/3 partycount, rounded down) @UUID[Compendium.night-city-gang-and-corp-mook-pack.NCGangsnCorpsMooks.Actor.iJLMcpMY01qaEAZI]{6th Street Technicals,} which are at 0 HP and are in the process of being stripped by scavengers. One of them is heavily on fire.

      • For each technical, place 2 dead @UUID[Compendium.night-city-gang-and-corp-mook-pack.NCGangsnCorpsMooks.Actor.qVFP250CBqed50XV]{6th Street Privates}, and 1 dead @UUID[Compendium.night-city-gang-and-corp-mook-pack.NCGangsnCorpsMooks.Actor.RJ8o6HbeyuCUsmxO]{6th Street Corporal}. Their corpses have already been picked over by the scavs for loot.

      • The emplacements provided by the privates can also be scattered around the scene at your discretion.

      • Also place 1 dead @UUID[Compendium.night-city-gang-and-corp-mook-pack.NCGangsnCorpsMooks.Actor.fr0YE5jG4hXccvQW]{6th Street Lieutenant} somewhere in the scene.

    • 1/2 partycount @UUID[Compendium.night-city-gang-and-corp-mook-pack.NCGangsnCorpsMooks.Actor.SNKnBqS4lCZPd5yU]{Scav Savages}, 1/3 partycount @UUID[Compendium.night-city-gang-and-corp-mook-pack.NCGangsnCorpsMooks.Actor.UM3c8a92eSdEQmB8]{Scav Monsters}, and 1*partycount Scav-aligned @UUID[Compendium.night-city-gang-and-corp-mook-pack.NCGangsnCorpsMooks.Actor.zeG9k6liLkdHNrf4]{Booster Gangsters} are in the lot, picking over the remains.

      • For every 6 scavs in the scene, add 1 scav-tagged @UUID[Compendium.night-city-gang-and-corp-mook-pack.NCGangsnCorpsMooks.Actor.skUkbMc9rOygWl4o]{Generic Van}, which is at 15 health and only has a max of 30.

      • The reinforcement boostergangers will all arrive in 2 rounds if alerted, and will arrive in a scav-tagged Generic Van which is at its max of 30 health.

      • If your party has an enemy in this mission, rename one of the scav monsters' token to that enemy's name.

  • Change the scene to @UUID[Scene.Wshcn0z64sESiqde]{South Night City Environment}.

    Then read the following:

Exterior, South Night City

The rain has finally tapered off, leaving a miserable grey sky that looks like it's ready to start crying again at any minute. Beneath its grey, depressed lighting, the dreary husks and ruins of SNC look apocalyptic. 6th Street Technicals patrol the streets, keeping watch for scavs, rival gangs, and the NCPD. Battered combat cabs crawl alongside them, kept company by well-armored couriers scurrying around on skateboards and bicycles. Thugs and the junkies prowl the sidewalks, and the homeless crowd alleyways and deserted ruins that have never been repaired from CW4. From a half a block away, you hear a shout: "Scott Brown motherfuckers, get the fuck out, I've got a showing!" Gunfire and panicked screaming follows shortly after.

The address that Vido gave you is in the heart of this urban decay - a pair of basketball courts outside the bullet-pocked shell of a high school, well into 6th Street territory and under their protections. The trickle of greasy smoke says otherwise.

(Change the scene to 6th Street Shootout.)

The 6th Streeters who were waiting to make the trade have been wiped out by a local scav gang. If your players charge into the scene or are made, the scavs are ready for them - skip to beat 3. Sprinter will not emerge from her room to assist in a firefight if she isn't contacted first.

Otherwise, any character with a base perception roll of 9 or more (or a DV9 perception check) can reveal that the 6th Streeters have been wiped out, and can do so from across the street to the north, where the scavs won't confront them. A DV15 perception check can give a ballpark estimate of how many scavs are present (I.E. "more than 5, less than 10); if the check is passed with an 18 or better, the exact count is provided.

Sprinter is watching for Vido's crew, and will signal the party by waving a flashlight at them out of her window.

Interior - James Martin Public School, Room 13

"Finally!" Sprinter says in a cockney accent as you arrive. "I was beginning to think that ol' Vido had forgotten about me!"

Tied to a chair behind her with a pulpy mass where his face used to be is a scav savage, stripped of his armor and weapons and groaning softly. The rest of the classroom is mundane; bare walls are lined with stacked tesks, and it's dark save for the window facing the street and the door at the far end of the room - although there's another window facing the court, its been blocked over with planks.

"Well then, now that you're 'ere, whats the plan?" she asks. The scav groans again, and she adds, "oh, right, introductions, then!"

Talking As Sprinter

Sprinter introduces herself as the Afterlife's concierge and acting head of security. The scav behind her is her interrogation target - she wants to know how the scavs got info on the meeting location to be able to grab the gear. She'll provide some barebones details about herself if anyone asks, but notes that now is not really the time.

  • Sprinter reveals that the chip containing the software the job needs is likely in one of the boxes/bags that the scavs have taken and put into the back of one of their vans, and just needs time to look for it.

    • To find the chip, an actor needs to be inside one of the vans and succeed a DV15 Conceal/Reveal object check as an action; this check can be repeated, if failed, and the chip can automatically be located after a combined 1 minute of searching.

  • Sprinter notes that 6th Street reinforcements will be here soon, and doesn't like the odds of getting out with the chip the gig needs

  • She's prepared to jump into the fray with the players, and will follow the party leader's plan so long as it doesn't put her unduly in harm's way or jeopardize the mission somehow.

  • She's about to give up on the scav behind her after spending some time trying to learn how they knew about the trade - but if the players can get some info out of them, she'll pass that along to Vido and they'll earn a bonus.

    • The info is worth an extra Eagle per player, if delivered.

    • Players can attempt to persuade or forcibly interrogate the thug to give up the information

      • If interrogated, the DV for the roll is set at 17. Depending on the methods used, you may want to reduce the thug's HP further and/or penalize the interrogating player with humanity loss.

      • If persuaded, the DV for the check is set at 17, and the player making the check makes it at a -2 penalty; this penalty can be removed if a DV15 first aid check is passed first, to help the thug's busted-up face.

    • If passed, the thug reveals that his boss knows a 6th Street insider, and provides the name of one Corporal Danielle Erins, the sister of whom the scavs took hostage a couple weeks ago.

      • If further pressured, the thug reveals that the sister was killed shortly after that, but Corporal Erins doesn't know.

    • The fate of the scav is in the players' hands - Sprinter is fine leaving him tied up, and has no intention of letting him go (or executing him).

Once players are ready to grab the software, or if the scavs are alerted (say, by a screaming and hollering scav hostage) proceed to beat 3.

In this beat, players must recover the chipware they're after from the scavs, one way or another.

The scavs in the basketball court are only really there to loot the software that they were after, which they've already found - at this point they're lounging about, keeping an eye on the streets for 6th Street reinforcements, looting the dead and waiting for orders.

You should be ready for this beat already, having set it up before the start of Beat 2.

The Win Condition

Players must acquire the chipsoft, by whatever means they feel appropriate.

  • The Chip Location

    • To find the chip, an actor needs to be inside one of the vans and succeed a DV15 Conceal/Reveal object check as an action; this check can be repeated, if failed, and the chip can automatically be located after a combined 1 minute of searching.

  • If players are feeling rich, they can attempt to just buy the chip in question; the scavs will ask for $1,250eb, reduced to $900eb if a DV15 trading/haggle check is passed.

    • If the check is failed, the scavs will immediately roll initiative and open fire, looking to take what the players have.

    • Note: Vido and the Afterlife have already paid for the chip once, and no amount of coercion will convince them to foot the extra cost.

    • If this route is taken, then when 6th Street arrives in Beat 4 they will assume the party is part of the scav gang and open fire on them; players can gun down the scavs alongside them, but will still need to persuade the streeters that they're not affiliated with the botched trade.

  • Stealthing past the scavs is also an option; a mook's "passive perception" is calculated as lowdown + 6, or lowdown + 3, any time the actor could potentially be seen.

    • Beyond that, how you rule stealth as a GM is up to you.

  • Otherwise, players will likely have to resort to combat.

    • The scavs will attempt to kill the party, up until more than 2/3rds are dead or seriously wounded. At that point, the survivors will attempt to get into the vans and fly off into the streets - if they make it to the front gate, the first one out crashes into the arriving 6th Streeters in encounter 4, blocking it.

    • If the scavs see that a party member has gone down, they won't prioritize that character, and instead choose to focus on anyone still up and fighting.

    • If the players are on the verge of defeat and Sprinter is still up, she will flee to the Afterlife and inform Vido that the job was a failure, likely climbing or jumping up across the rooftop of the classroom she had been interrogating her prisoner in to do so.

    • If the party fails the gig somehow, Vido doesn't hold it against any survivors, but notes that a failure on their audition job isn't exactly a glowing recommendation.

      • On failure, the questline will end until the players purchase the chip for Vido on the black market for $1,500eb - alternately, Vido will shrug, the Afterlife will buy the chip from 6th Street again, and you can proceed with the next mission regardless. No rewards except IP should be given to the survivors.

Once the chip is recovered, Sprinter will take possession of it immediately for safe keeping.

Beat 4 commences any of the following occurs:

  • The scavs finish looting the scene and attempt to leave;

  • 1 minute passes after the scavs are defeated by the players;

  • The scavs attempt to flee the scene after being confronted, and a van makes it to the front gate;

  • 10 seconds pass after the players acquire the security chipsoft under any circumstances.

In this beat, 6th Street reinforcements arrive to figure out what happened during the deal. Players will need to contend with them.

  • Place 1 @UUID[Compendium.night-city-gang-and-corp-mook-pack.NCGangsnCorpsMooks.Actor.iJLMcpMY01qaEAZI]{6th Street Technical} on the road heading towards the school, as well as 1/3 partycount (rounded down) @UUID[Compendium.night-city-gang-and-corp-mook-pack.NCGangsnCorpsMooks.Actor.NcmoLFKKy8hGryfN]{Generic Compacts}.

    • The technical is manned by 1 @UUID[Compendium.night-city-gang-and-corp-mook-pack.NCGangsnCorpsMooks.Actor.RJ8o6HbeyuCUsmxO]{6th Street Corporal}, 1 @UUID[Compendium.night-city-gang-and-corp-mook-pack.NCGangsnCorpsMooks.Actor.fr0YE5jG4hXccvQW]{6th Street Lieutenant}, and 1 6th-Street Aligned @UUID[Compendium.night-city-gang-and-corp-mook-pack.NCGangsnCorpsMooks.Actor.siXtBrzjkX0N0AIc]{Gangster Enforcer}.

      • Rename the Lieutenant to Lt. Sam Maxwell

    • Each Compact Groundcar is additionally manned by 2 @UUID[Compendium.night-city-gang-and-corp-mook-pack.NCGangsnCorpsMooks.Actor.qVFP250CBqed50XV]{6th Street Privates} and 1 6th-Street Aligned @UUID[Compendium.night-city-gang-and-corp-mook-pack.NCGangsnCorpsMooks.Actor.zeG9k6liLkdHNrf4]{Booster Gangster.}

      • Gangster reinforcements will arrive as a unit on foot from the road 1d3 rounds after being summoned by 6th Street, and can be summoned as an action by the Enforcer, Corporal or Lieutenant at GM discretion.

Then read the following:

6th Street Goes To War

You hear a rumble of tires and overtaxed engines - from the road, lights flash. Another 6th Street Technical leads the charge as reinforcements for the dead gang members on the lot roar down the street, and the turret at the top of the technical is manned, loaded, and ready to fire.

(If the scavs got to a van and tried to flee):

There's a sudden screech and a blaring horn - a moment later, a sound of crashing metal as the van collides with the technical. You hear a crunch, a brief scream, and another crunch, presumably as the scav driver is ejected through the windshield. The van itself is suddenly immobilized in the gate, blocking access in or out.

(Regardless)

Someone's shouting over a microphone from the road: "6th Street! Drop your weapons or we will open fire!"

Running Lieutenant Maxwell

The LT. that's leading the group is named Sam Maxwell; the corporal in the technical is Danielle Erins, the mole inside 6th Street (she has loyalty to 6th Street and will not betray them for the scavs, no matter what the scavs say or attempt).

  • If any scavs are still alive in the scene, they will attempt to attack the Streeters; the Streeters will respond by opening fire, and will assume that the party is part of the group that attacked and killed their original trading group.

  • Otherwise, 6th Street will demand the party drop their weapons. If they comply, 6th Street will strip the party of their possessions and interrogate them on the lot.

    • Unless more than half the party failed the personal grooming check that they made for beat 1, in which case they'll assume the party are scavs and will open fire regardless.

  • A DV17 Persuasion check can be made to convince Lieutenant Maxwell that the party is not affiliated with the scavs. This check is made with a -2 penalty if 6th Street is currently shooting at them, and a +2 bonus if the party participates in killing any remaining living scavs, and/or turns over the still-living hostage in the classroom, and/or reveals that Corporal Erins is the mole if they have that information.

    • A DV15 persuasion check can be made instead to try and persuade Maxwell to let them call Vido, or to have Maxwell do it. Vido will confirm that they work for the Afterlife. If failed, Maxwell assumes they're trying to call more reinforcements.

      • If players remembered the codephrase from earlier ("Remmy wants a roast beef sandwich"), the check is auto-passed.

    • If this check is failed, but players attempt no hostile action, Maxwell will ultimately decide not to kill them, and Vido can later persuade him to hand over the chip, making the mission a long-term success assuming the party survives beat 5.

      • He will, however, confiscate any weapons, and any gear worth 100eb or more that the party is carrying on their person; items worth 100eb or less will be in Vido's possession 3 days later, but 6th Street will keep anything more expensive than that for themselves.

    • If Erins is revealed when the persuasion check is passed, she breaks down and confesses in tears; Maxwell cites the dead 6th Streeters on the court and places her under military arrest to await tribunal.

    • If Erins is revealed after the persuasion check is passed, she breaks down to much the same result and Lieutenant Maxwell thanks the players, offering them a reward of any set of non-exotic weapons & ammo from the 6th Street Arsenal totalling out at 200eb or less.

      • If players testify to the circumstances, she will survive and contact the players a day after the job finishes, informing them she's been "dishonorably discharged" but that their character witness was what kept her from being executed, and provide them with a total of $150eb as thank you.

      • Otherwise, she will later be executed.

  • Players can also opt to fight their way out; aside from the gangsters, no further reinforcements from 6th Street arrive.

Regardless of how the situation is resolved, if/when the players leave the scene with the chipsoft in their possession, read the following:

A Mad Dash

You run pell-mell through the streets of Night City, leaving the combat zone behind you. It seems the scavs haven't forgotten you, however, and evidently they know where you're going. You see several more affiliated vans roaming the streets, though none manage to spot you (barely) on your return.

(If she's with the party)

Sprinter is counting the blocks - "alright, just five more. Four more now. Three to go…"

Finally, the doors of the Afterlife are in sight - and accompanied by the screech of tires that echo off the surrounding buildings. Scav vehicles appear at either side of the street and burn rubber as they screech towards you. "Come on, go, go, run!" Sprinter shouts. Ahead of you, the doors are already open.

(Proceed to beat 5.)

In this beat, players will need to defend the Afterlife from angry Scavs and/or 6th Streeters who've come to take revenge.

This beat takes place in the @UUID[Scene.s4clZm7hY4mfQllb]{Afterlife Lobby} scene, and commences after players have left the 6th Street Shootout scene with the chipsoft they're after in their possession. Regardless of their standing with 6th Street, it's the scavs that end up pursuing them most doggedly - unless players somehow allied with the scavs and took out 6th Street.

If that's the case, substitute the generic vans for generic trucks with the same stats, and any Scav Monsters with an even mix of 6th Street Privates and Corporals, then continue as planned.

Prep

  • Have each player make a MOVE save (roll 1d10 - a result ≤ their MOVE characteristic passes).

    • For any player which passes their save, their token is placed just inside the doors to the Afterlife.

    • For any player which fails, they're placed 10 spaces from the front doors, on the sidewalk.

  • Place @UUID[Actor.C1opBA4gYUZcTiGp]{Sprinter's} token just inside the doors to the Afterlife, in front of anyone who passed their check.

  • @UUID[Actor.17CTAE5lKmZFHY9i]{Vido's} token is in the same place he was when the players first met him.

  • Place 1/2 partycount @UUID[Compendium.night-city-gang-and-corp-mook-pack.NCGangsnCorpsMooks.Actor.UM3c8a92eSdEQmB8]{Scav Monsters}, any scav survivors of the 6th Street Shootout scene, and 1*partycount scav-aligned @UUID[Compendium.night-city-gang-and-corp-mook-pack.NCGangsnCorpsMooks.Actor.zeG9k6liLkdHNrf4]{Booster Gangsters} in the scene.

    • No reinforcements are summoned for the (Dis)Organized Crime ability.

    • For every 6 enemies placed, create 1 scav-tagged Generic Van with a max of 30 health.

    • Evenly distribute enemies to arrive from either side of the scene, if possible.

  • Switch the scene to @UUID[Scene.s4clZm7hY4mfQllb]{Afterlife Lobby}

  • Remind players of the mission's personal rep opportunity

    Then read the following:

Triumphant Returns(?)

Sprinter bursts through the lobby doors in a run befitting her name - out of breath, but likely doing better than most anyone else would. "Got it!" she shouts to Vido. "Scavs incoming! Shut the door!"

Vido's eyes glow for a moment, and then he swears as he pulls out his pistol from his jacket. "Merde - it's not working! Go, plug it in!"

Sprinter dashes off and takes an abrupt right at the concierge's desk, disappearing into the hotel.

"All right, mis vachers!" Vido exclaims as he levels his gun. "If you are feeling down, put on a smile, if you are hurt, rub some dirt on it - this is what I pay you for!

(Roll initiative.)

Defend The Afterlife!

As soon as initiative is rolled, the scavs will attempt to charge the building. The party's goal is to defend the entrance long enough for Sprinter to make it to admin, install the new software and raise the building's defenses.

This process takes one minute, or after Stage 2 completes - whichever is first.

Stage 1 (Arrival)

  • As soon as initiative is rolled, the van driver(s) will attempt to ram any players remaining outside.

    • If the van hits the building by accident, or hits one of the streetlamps, it suffers 6d6 damage; the building is not damaged unless it is deliberately rammed (see stage 3).

    • To dodge being hit, players can attempt a contested evasion check vs the van driver's technical roll; on success, the van misses them and continues moving.

    • If any players are hit, the scavs don't stop to double-tap - a deduction base roll of 11 or more is enough to determine that playing dead would work, so long as they aren't accidentally run over in the process.

  • Once they're close enough, the van(s) occupants will dismount, attempting to storm the entrance while finishing off any players left outside.

    • The occuptants will storm in pairs from either side of the door, and can be repelled - any scav who takes more than 7 damage before finding cover inside is considered repelled, as are any targets which are suppressed.

    • Once the first pair is gunned down or repelled, proceed to stage 2

Stage 2 (Breaching)

  • If any vans are still operational, a scav will bark an order, and the closest mook to one will return to it and attempt to ram the building, which requires 1 action to start and 1 action to complete, and only one van attempts it at a time.

    • On success, the manages to push through the front of the building in a cloud of rubble, and pulls free before breaking down completely, leaving a hole in what was formerly tinted thick bulletproof glass.

    • Vans can be stopped from ramming the building; a DV13 tactics check or a base tactics score of 11 or more is sufficient to deduce that doing so can be accomplished by:

      • Shooting and hitting one or more tires

      • Shooting and hitting the ganger behind the wheel

      • Dealing more than half of the van's health in damage in one round.

      • Repelled vans will lose control and crash, lopsided, in front of the building.

  • If no vans are operational, the scavs will attempt to shoot out the glass at the front of the building instead; once a pane is broken (one space), some will attempt to enter, while others widen the gap.

  • Once the scavs have gotten inside, or if every van is disabled/unable to ram a window, proceed to stage 3.

Stage 3 (Overrun)

Read the following:

"I got it!" Sprinter's cockney accent shouts over the building's internal speakers. Lights flash - the hotel lobby suddenly goes from blue to red as security systems activate. The hum and whine of automated defenses activating is accompanied by reinforced steel security shutters clattering down over the building's front windows. "Eat this!" the woman shouts, as chaos breaks loose.

Place an @UUID[Actor.nWA22iuCxIViNuE2]{Afterlife Security Turret} above the outside entrance; place another just inside the doors, on the wall above (4m up). Place two more 5 meters up, on either side of the wall behind the concierge's desk, between the stairwell and the elevators. Place a final one above the garage entrance 5 meters up, on the left side of the scene.

  • If scavs have breached the building, these turrets do not activate for 2 more rounds.

    • Otherwise, they immediately activate.

Assign each turret to a unique player; until the scene ends, that player may control the turret's action during their character's turn, in addition to their own. (Note: Sprinter is controlling the turrets, this is just more fun).

  • Metal shutters clatter shut on the front walls; any breaches in the lobby's walls are repaired immediately.

  • Additionally, at the start of every round, Vido may now choose to open/close the front door of the Afterlife. He may also do so as an action.

    • Anyone in the doorway when this happens must make a reflex save

      • On success, they may move to either side of the doorway.

      • On failure, they suffer 4d6 damage (reduced by body armor), a random appendage is severed (they take the damage for the critical injury), and they're placed on whichever side of the doorway they were closer to when it shut.

  • The scavs will retreat if more than 2/3rds their number is killed, critically injured or seriously wounded, or if they do not destroy at least one turret every other round.

Once the scavs have been driven off, proceed to the conclusion.

If No Scavs Made It Into The Lobby…

The sudden silence is eerie. Lights flash across the lobby. A minute later, it returns to its normal coloration; a cool, calm and artificial female voice says over the loudspeakers, "security breach contained. Thank you for your patience."

"Well!" Vido says, standing up and dusting his hands. "A little bit messy, but all in all, another day in Night City, no?"

(If a player completed the mission's personal rep opportunity, Vido takes the time to compliment him in particular.)

"Now then, I imagine you are in need of payment, and I am in need of a drink. I know a lovely bar in South Night City that will serve us well to one end, and the strongbox I keep in its basement will serve well to the other." Raising his voice, he adds, "come, Sprinter! I have not yet seen you drunk, and am curious if it betters your British nature."

If Scavs DID Make It In…

Dust trickles to the ground as the last of the scavs scurries away. Vido sighs and winces as a bit of plaster falls from the walls, and the turrets are still humming, looking for active targets.

"Well mes amies, I hope you don't mind me saying, that could have gone better."

He pauses to further inspect the scene, and winces again. "Aaaah, and they just put in those carpets…"

"Still, a job is a job, and you played your role admirably. No hard feelings for the property damage, eh? Perhaps insurance will cover it; 'acts of savagery' are as common clauses as 'acts of God,' these days."

"Now then, I imagine you are in need of payment, and I am in need of a drink. I know a lovely bar in South Night City that will serve us well to one end, and the strongbox I keep in its basement will serve well to the other." Raising his voice, he adds, "come, Sprinter! I have not yet seen you drunk, and am curious if it betters your British nature."

Rewards should now be dispensed according to the mission summary - pay special mind to any outstanding extras the players might have earned, like by turning in (then defending) Corporal Erins.

Vido is overall satisfied with any ending that sees the hotel's defenses activate, and a short scene at the bar (the included Retrograde Pool Bar scene can be used for ambience) is an excellent opportunity to introduce a moment where Sprinter befriends any of the edgerunners that are of interest to her.

Once the session has concluded, the players unlock the first service of the Afterlife. Details about it can be found in the @UUID[JournalEntry.yt2ffG5B0uqTKi1z]{Services Of The Afterlife Journals, Journal 1. Entrance Fee - Sanctuary & Reputation.} If you haven't already, you should also make the associated journal visible to your players - you can do so by right clicking it, selecting "Configure Ownership", and changing the "All Players" dropdown to "Observer."

Overall, Vido is sure to note that he will have more work available for the party as soon as they are ready.

Your Feedback Wanted!

Did you enjoy this session? Did something go wrong? Was there broken content? I would very much like to hear about your successes and failures both.

If you have a few minutes after your session, please take the time to fill out the feedback survey for this campaign, located here. It should only take 5 minutes, and will be actively considered as I make revisions to the campaign.

Your feedback matters! Thank you for playing!

(Nightlight is a recurring character who will be important later, and adds context for the player's actions - read out her editorial at the start of the session, or post it in your group chat!)

The FM Receiver Crackles, The Pirate Wavelength Carrying A Cool, Seductive Female Voice…

"Welcome back my dear Night Owls. This is Nightlight, the voice of the city, here to take the scream off the sheet and into your ears for all our Night Citizens without enough time to read.

"How are we feeling tonight? Tired as you wind down from the day? Groggy as you wake up for the night? Either way Night Citizens, let's hope you get where you need to go. Time for some news.

"Scavenger presence in NC has been on a steep incline, with claims from night citizens that they 'keep multiplying day by day'. Either the friendly neighborhood chop-shop makes enough scratch to call our dear Night Citizens to their cause, people are gettin' desperate in the 15th straight year of depression, or something's pulling them in from out in the badlands; maybe they just couldn't take the taste of sand in their mouths any longer. What do you think?

"In lighter news: I got a message for our edgerunning community. I have it on good authority that something big is on the way - something so big, not even Nightlight can spoil it. All I can say is that it looks like some of you solos will be making it to heaven after all, in the next few months. Lucky you.

"Lastly, the NCPD wants to remind all of you that those cadavers in the gutter are not toys. Please stop giving children human remains. If you spot a body on the street, or anyone providing a child with that spare arm they had lying around, please contact the NCPD. If you see something, say something.

"Batty Karlson's up next, talking about 'whether or not the great bozo was an alien this whole time'. Sounds fun.

"Until later Night Citizens, this is Nightlight, hoping I made your night a little brighter."
